2605.12185.title: Mitigating Context-Memory Conflicts in LLMs through Dynamic Cognitive Reconciliation Decoding
2605.12185.paper_summary: Large language models accumulate extensive parametric knowledge through pre-training. However, knowledge conflicts occur when outdated or incorrect parametric knowledge conflicts with external knowledge in the context. Existing methods address knowledge conflicts through contrastive decoding, but in conflict-free scenarios, static approaches disrupt output distribution. Other dynamic decoding methods attempt to measure the degree of conflict but still struggle with complex real-world situations. In this paper, we propose a two-stage decoding method called Dynamic Cognitive Reconciliation Decoding (DCRD), to predict and mitigate context-memory conflicts. DCRD first analyzes the attention map to assess context fidelity and predict potential conflicts. Based on this prediction, the input is directed to one of two decoding paths: (1) greedy decoding, or (2) context fidelity-based dynamic decoding. This design enables DCRD to handle conflicts efficiently while maintaining high accuracy and decoding efficiency in conflict-free cases. Additionally, to simulate scenarios with frequent knowledge updates, we constructed ConflictKG, a knowledge conflict QA benchmark. Experiments on four LLMs across six QA datasets show that DCRD outperforms all baselines, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
2605.12185.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型透過預訓練積累了大量的參數知識。<lb/>然而，當過時或不正確的參數知識與上下文中的外部知識發生衝突時，知識衝突就會出現。<lb/>現有的方法通過對比解碼來解決知識衝突，但在無衝突的情況下，靜態方法會擾亂輸出分佈。<lb/>其他動態解碼方法試圖衡量衝突的程度，但在複雜的現實情況中仍然面臨困難。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種名為動態認知調和解碼（DCRD）的兩階段解碼方法，以預測和減輕上下文記憶衝突。<lb/>DCRD首先分析注意力圖以評估上下文的真實性並預測潛在的衝突。<lb/>根據這一預測，輸入將被引導至兩條解碼路徑中的一條：（1）貪婪解碼，或（2）基於上下文真實性的動態解碼。<lb/>這一設計使DCRD能夠有效處理衝突，同時在無衝突的情況下保持高準確性和解碼效率。<lb/>此外，為了模擬頻繁知識更新的場景，我們構建了ConflictKG，一個知識衝突問答基準。<lb/>在四個大型語言模型和六個問答數據集上的實驗顯示，DCRD超越了所有基準，達到了最先進的性能。
2605.12185.author: Yigeng Zhou et.al.
2605.12185.authors: Yigeng Zhou, Wu Li, Yifan Lu, Yequan Wang, Xuebo Liu, Wenya Wang, Jun Yu, Min Zhang, Jing Li
